text:
LAREDO, Tex. (KGNS) - Laredo can now claim to have had an Olympian compete for Team USA, as Jennifer “La Traviesa” Lozano stepped into the ring Thursday, August 1, at the Paris Games.

From the opening bell, Lozano appeared uneasy, possibly due to her long layoff and the bright lights of the Olympic stage. Her Finnish opponent had a clear size advantage, which Lozano struggled to overcome.

Throughout the first round, the Finnish boxer used her length effectively, keeping Lozano at bay and making it difficult for her to score. Lozano’s troubles continued into the second round, where she again struggled to generate offense and lost the round.

Entering the final round, Lozano needed a substantial comeback to advance. Despite a determined effort, the judges awarded the victory to her opponent, ending Lozano’s Olympic aspirations.

The defeat was met with a wave of support from Laredo, where watch parties and social media buzzed with pride for their Olympian. Residents gathered to cheer on Lozano, regardless of the outcome.

Dr. Victor Trevino, the mayor of Laredo, expressed his honor at Lozano’s participation. “It is important because she represents Laredo as one of our best. We have good talent here.”

Jamie Moyer, a professor at TAMIU, praised Lozano’s prospects. “She’s on the Olympic team, which means she’s one of the best in the country. She certainly has a career ahead of her.”

Despite the sting of defeat, Lozano’s close friends remain supportive. “We have been waiting for this moment,” said Brenda Gutierrez. “She has made her dreams come true. I have seen it all from the very beginning.”

Cristina Ramos added, “I am a little sad, but I know Jenny is going to come through with this. She is going to take this as something to learn from.”

Support for Lozano continues to grow. Yalitza Howard, a supporter, emphasized the importance of backing her. “It’s important to follow her journey and support her in ways we can.”

Paulo Lozano, another supporter, highlighted the significance of Lozano’s achievement. “It’s very important for them to see that if you are passionate and determined, you can achieve significant goals, like going to the Olympics and putting Laredo on the map.”

Though Lozano will return home without a gold medal, she will come back knowing she gave her all. While she is not scheduled to fight again in Paris, Lozano is expected back in Laredo in about a week and a half. She may consider a return to Team USA for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles or explore turning professional.

title: Jennifer Lozano’s Olympic journey ends in defeat, but Laredo’s pride remains
description: Jennifer Lozano’s run at the Paris Games came to an end Thursday as she fell to her Finnish opponent in a unanimous decision.
keywords: laredo,laredo news,kgns,jennifer lozano,olympics,olympic games,paris,france,finnish,opponent,unanimous decision,loss,troubles,defeat,watch parties,social media,olympian,la traviesa,talent,usa,supporters
